prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a rast has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the rast-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a rast, a plon, and a gox. The rast is underneath the plon. The plon is to-the-left-of the gox. The rast has a purple button on top, a yellow button on side, and a red button on bottom. The plon has a yellow button on top and a purple button on bottom. The gox has a red button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the rast activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a florp, a wix, and a fro. The fro is underneath the wix. The wix is to-the-left-of the florp. The florp has a red button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The wix has a purple button on top, a purple button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The fro has a purple button on top, a yellow button on side, and a red button on bottom. The wix's purple button is described in the catalog as its primary control.

Which object in the perception scenario is the rast-analog, and which button activates it?
